metaDescription: Find private student accommodation near UNSW in Kensington. Per-room rentals in share houses close to campus, the L3 light rail on Anzac Parade, and Kingsford's shops. Browse rooms on Quni Living.
intro: The University of New South Wales sits at the eastern end of Anzac Parade in Kensington, with its main Kensington Campus bordered by High Street to the south and surrounded by some of Sydney's most liveable suburbs. Whether you grab a room on a quiet street off Todman Avenue or further along Anzac Parade toward Kingsford, you'll be within a short walk or light rail ride of lectures, libraries, and the Roundhouse. Quni Living lists private, per-room student housing across Kensington and nearby suburbs so you can find a place that fits your budget and lifestyle without signing a whole-property lease.

livingSection.heading: What It's Like Living in Kensington as a Student
livingSection.body: Kensington is a compact, predominantly residential suburb shaped around Anzac Parade, which doubles as the local high street with supermarkets, cafés, bakeries, and takeaway spots lining the stretch between the university gates and the Kingsford shopping strip to the south. Day-to-day errands are easy — there's a Coles on Anzac Parade at Kingsford and an array of Asian grocers and restaurants along the same corridor. Centennial Park sits just to the west of Kensington, offering running tracks, cycling paths, and wide-open green space for weekend downtime. Heading east or southeast, Coogee Beach and Maroubra Beach are both reachable by bus, giving you a proper coastal lifestyle alongside your studies. The suburb itself is quiet and flat, and most residential streets between Anzac Parade and Doncaster Avenue are an easy walk to campus. Neighbouring Randwick adds extra shopping and dining options around The Spot on St Pauls Street, while Maroubra offers slightly more affordable room options with a beach on your doorstep.

transportSection.heading: Getting Around from Kensington
transportSection.body: The L3 Kingsford light rail line is the backbone of transport for UNSW students. It runs along Anzac Parade with a dedicated stop at UNSW, connecting the campus to Kingsford to the south and running north through Moore Park and Surry Hills into the CBD at Circular Quay. The light rail means you don't need to rely on buses to reach the city centre, though several bus routes also run along Anzac Parade and Alison Road linking Kensington to Randwick, Coogee, Maroubra, and Central Station. If you live in Kingsford or southern Kensington, you can hop on the L3 at any of the stops along Anzac Parade and be at the UNSW stop within minutes. Cycling is also practical — the terrain is largely flat between Kensington, Kingsford, and Randwick, and bike storage is available across the Kensington Campus. For weekend trips further afield, Central Station is a short light rail ride away, connecting you to Greater Sydney's train network.

costSection.heading: Understanding Rental Costs near UNSW
costSection.body: Accommodation costs around Kensington vary depending on what you're renting. A room in a share house — the most common arrangement for students near UNSW — typically costs less than a self-contained studio or one-bedroom apartment, because you're splitting common areas and utility costs with housemates. Studios and one-bedrooms along Anzac Parade or in Randwick command a premium because they offer full privacy and their own kitchen and bathroom. In a per-room let through Quni Living, rent often includes furnishings and may bundle internet and some utilities, which simplifies budgeting compared to a whole-property lease where you arrange and pay for electricity, gas, water, and broadband separately. Under NSW residential tenancy law, a rental bond is capped at four weeks' rent for weekly rents under a certain threshold and is lodged with NSW Fair Trading rather than held by the landlord. In share house or room-by-room arrangements, the bond structure can differ slightly — some room agreements are licence agreements rather than formal leases — so always clarify whether your bond will be lodged with Fair Trading and what the notice period is before signing. Per-room lets price differently from whole-property leases because the landlord or head tenant absorbs vacancy risk on individual rooms, and furnishings and bills bundling add convenience value. This usually means the per-week rate for a single room is higher than dividing a whole-house lease evenly, but the trade-off is flexibility, lower upfront costs, and fewer administrative headaches.

tipsSection.heading: Tips for Finding a Room near UNSW
tips: Look along and just off Anzac Parade between the UNSW campus gates and the Kingsford shops — this corridor has the highest density of share houses and is served by every L3 light rail stop, so you can walk to lectures or jump on at the UNSW stop in minutes.

Consider rooms in Maroubra or Randwick if Kensington itself is too competitive. Both suburbs have frequent bus connections along Anzac Parade and Maroubra Road, and Randwick's shops around The Spot add a strong local village feel.

Inspect whether your room agreement is a formal residential tenancy or a licence agreement — in NSW, this affects your bond protections and your rights around notice periods and rent increases. Ask the landlord directly and request written confirmation before paying any money.

faqs.question: What transport options are available for student accommodation near UNSW, Kensington?
faqs.answer: The L3 Kingsford light rail line runs along Anzac Parade with a stop at UNSW, connecting you directly to Kingsford to the south and the CBD to the north via Moore Park and Surry Hills. Multiple bus routes also operate along Anzac Parade and Alison Road, linking Kensington to Randwick, Coogee, Maroubra, and Central Station. The area is flat and well-suited to cycling.

faqs.question: Which suburbs are best for student accommodation near UNSW, Kensington?
faqs.answer: Kensington itself is the most convenient, particularly streets between Anzac Parade and Doncaster Avenue. Kingsford is popular because it's one or two light rail stops south along Anzac Parade and has a busy shopping strip with affordable dining. Randwick offers a village atmosphere around The Spot, while Maroubra provides beach access and often slightly lower room rates.

faqs.question: How do per-room rentals work for student accommodation near UNSW, Kensington?
faqs.answer: Per-room rentals let you lease a single furnished bedroom in a share house without signing a whole-property lease. Rent often includes furnishings and may bundle utilities and internet. You pay a bond — typically up to four weeks' rent — which under a formal NSW tenancy must be lodged with Fair Trading. This setup gives you flexibility to move at the end of your agreement without being responsible for finding replacement tenants for the entire property.

ctaText: Browse per-room student accommodation near UNSW in Kensington, Kingsford, Randwick, and Maroubra. Find your room on Quni Living today.
